Off Calendar Oct-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRIAN H. BUDDELL, Esq. of Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP (Tel: 415-949-1900) for Defendants/Cross-Complainants George N. Mainas and Maria K. Mainas;
Atty. ASHLEY N. MEYERS, Esq. of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman + Scheley
(Tel: 925-734-0990) for Cross-Defendants Andrea Da Vis and Michael Da Vis.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS GEORGE MAINAS AND MARIA MAINAS' Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Cross-Defendant Andrea Davis And Request For Sanctions Against Andrea Davis And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $1910.00.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows:

Despite the technical deficiency in moving party's separate statement, the Court finds opposing party's objections and arguments lacking merit. The Court grants the motion and orders Opposing party Cross-Defendant Anglea Davis to appear at a continued deposition at a mutually convenient time and place for the parties (parties have agreed to December 3, 2020) and provide full and complete answers to the following questions: 1. "And the incident itself involved a decorative balcony that came loose from the front of the building and caused two women to be injured, correct?; 2. "So you don't understand what the term 'decorative' means?"; 3. "Based on your personal knowledge and nothing else, do you believe you have any responsibility for the injuries that were sustained by Ms. Shiba or Ms. Useche on that night?"; 4. "So why would it be important to you to run this statement by him [referring to deponent's father], given he has no personal information about the facts of the accident?"; 5. "How do you know Mr. McNeil?"; 6. "What was the purpose for which you contacted Mr. McNeil?"; 7. "What was your understanding as to the type of service Mr. McNeil was to provide for you?"; 8. "When is your understanding as to when the attorney/client privilege began with you and Mr. McNeil?"; 9. "When you spoke with Mr. McNeil, did you have the understanding that he was representing not only you but both your parents?"; 10. "When you retained Mr. McNeil as an attorney, who was paying his fee?"; 11. "Have you ever received a bill from Mr. McNeil for his services?"; 12. "So you retained the services of Mr. McNeil without knowing what firm he worked at; is that right?"; 13. "At some point you decided to switch attorneys from Mr. McNeil to Mr. Beeman's office, correct?"; 14. "When did you switch representation to Mr. Beeman's firm?"; 15. "Have you received a bill from Mr. Beeman's office for his services?"

The Court also finds moving party has met and conferred sufficiently in good faith because the parties acknowledged that they were at an impasse in the meet and confer process, any further meet and confer would have been futile. In lieu of the Court awarding monetary sanctions Cross-Defendant Angela Davis and her counsel, the parties have agreed that the Clapp Moroney firm is to pay the court reporter and transcript costs for the second session of Ms. Davis' deposition.

The Court further finds that opposing party has not established that moving party engaged in discovery in a manner or to an extent that causes unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense. If opposing party truly believed that the moving party's questions was a misuse of the discovery process, opposing party could have suspended the deposition and sought a protective order. The Court therefore is inclined to deny opposing party Cross-Defendant Angela Davis' request for sanctions against moving party and their counsel.

Cross-complainants to prepare and email a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
